differential_diagnoses: Common variable immunodeficiency (CVID) with associated enteropathy
Primary intestinal lymphoma (e.g., indolent T-cell lymphoproliferative disorder of the GI tract)
next_steps: Surgical consultation for possible exploratory laparotomy or resection of symptomatic jejunal segments
Deep enteroscopy (balloon-assisted) with repeat targeted biopsies of the jejunal wall and masses
Flow cytometry and T-cell receptor (TCR) gene rearrangement studies on biopsy tissue to evaluate for occult T-cell lymphoma
Review of pathology slides by an expert hematopathologist with specialized immunohistochemistry (CD3, CD4, CD8, CD56, CD30, TCR-beta, TCR-gamma, EBER)
Evaluation for occult systemic lymphoma (PET/CT scan)
Genetic counseling and testing (e.g., CTLA4, LRBA mutations) for underlying common variable immunodeficiency (CVID) etiology

differential_diagnoses: Indolent T-cell lymphoproliferative disorder of the gastrointestinal tract
Common variable immunodeficiency (CVID) with systemic lymphoproliferative complications
Crohn's disease with secondary intestinal lymphoma
next_steps: Surgical consultation for resection of symptomatic, ischemic jejunal segments with mural aneurysmal dilatation
Full-thickness histopathologic and immunohistochemical evaluation of resected bowel (CD3, CD4, CD8, CD56, CD30, TCR-beta, TCR-gamma, EBER, Ki-67)
T-cell receptor (TCR) gene rearrangement studies on fresh tissue and blood
Bone marrow biopsy and aspiration with flow cytometry to assess for clonal NK/T-cell expansion
Whole-body PET/CT scan for staging and identification of potential lymphomatous sites
Genetic testing for primary immunodeficiency markers including CTLA4, LRBA, and PI3K pathway mutations
Repeat serum protein electrophoresis and free light chain assay to track monoclonal gammopathy
